title: UI Polish Phase 2 — Visual Hierarchy & Screen Consistency
problem: Every screen was built independently and lacks visual cohesion — font scales, spacing, and color usage are inconsistent. The main menu has no dungeon atmosphere. Combat log is plain monochrome text. The grid dungeon tiles are grey boxes. Item cards show no rarity color in the grid. There are no screen transitions — cuts are jarring. The overall aesthetic doesn't match the dark fantasy tone of a Diablo-inspired game.
analysis: All screens share #0a0a0a background but use locally-defined styles with inconsistent spacing (padding ranges from 14 to 20), mixed font sizes (10-22px in ad-hoc increments), and no shared color constants. GridScreen tiles are plain grey TouchableOpacity boxes with no visual distinction by tile type. CombatScreen monster name is always #e74c3c red regardless of type. TetrisGrid renders items as colored squares with no rarity border. Main menu is 3 plain buttons. No screen transitions between any screens.
implementation: 1. src/theme.ts — export COLORS (bg, surface, border, text primary/secondary/dim, quality colors, class colors), FONT (xs=9, sm=11, md=13, lg=16, xl=20, xxl=26), SPACING (xs=4, sm=8, md=14, lg=20, xl=28). Used as import in all screens for consistency. 2. GridScreen.tsx — tile visual overhaul: unexplored tiles show unicode stone chars ('▓','░','▒') in dark grey. Boss tile: red border + skull emoji. Chest tile: yellow border + chest emoji. Exit tile: green border + ↓ arrow. Fog of war: tiles beyond 2 steps from player position render at 30% opacity. Floor + tier header badge: top bar showing 'FLOOR 7 · TIER II' in proper typography. 3. CombatScreen.tsx — monster name color varies by type: Normal=#999, Elite=#3498db, Rare=#c8a020, Ancient=#e74c3c, Boss=#ff0000 + pulsing border. Boss header adds ☠ skull before name + 'BOSS' badge. Combat log lines: 4 most recent shown, older fade to near-invisible. 4. App.tsx MainMenu — title 'DUNGEON DEPTHS' with letter-spacing:8, subtitle 'A DIABLO-STYLE ROGUELITE', animated border pulse on the card. Class dot indicator: shows current classId color if a class is active. 5. LootScreen.tsx — item cards get quality-colored left border (2px). Rare/unique items get a subtle animated shimmer (Animated opacity oscillation 0.7→1 loop). 6. TetrisGrid (ItemCard) — potion items show 🧪 or 💧 centered in their 1×1 cell instead of a plain color block. Rune items show their rune initial (El, Eld...) in gold. 7. Screen transitions — add a shared useFadeTransition hook: Animated.Value opacity 0→1 on mount over 180ms. Apply to all screen root Views. 8. EquipmentPanel.tsx — slot labels are more descriptive, empty slots show a faint icon hint (⚔ for weapon, 🛡 for offhand etc).

decisions.decision: Visual polish pass 2 targets InventoryScreen, CombatScreen, GridScreen consistency
decisions.rationale: These three screens are the most frequently visited; consistency here has the highest player impact.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: MF (magic find) stat line added to InventoryScreen between vitals and skills strip — shows LVL N and MF N% in gold
decisions.rationale: Magic find is an invisible stat that drives loot decisions; surfacing it in the inventory gives players actionable build feedback.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: DPad center converted from non-interactive View to TouchableOpacity with onLongPress (400ms) — accepts onWait prop
decisions.rationale: Long-press is a discoverable interaction pattern that avoids cluttering the grid UI with an explicit wait button.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: potion heal in InventoryScreen reads effectiveStats.heal (floor-scaled) instead of hardcoded 30 — both handleBagEquip and handleSheetUse updated
decisions.rationale: Floor-scaled potions were implemented in loot.ts but InventoryScreen was still applying a flat 30 HP heal, breaking the floor scaling.

successCriteria: All screens use theme.ts constants. Grid tiles are visually distinct by type. Monster names are color-coded by encounter type. Main menu has atmospheric title treatment. Potion/rune items show icons in inventory grid. Screen fades on transition.
knownLimitations: DPad long-press wait triggers haptic only — no game-state turn-skip mechanic yet
componentFile: src/theme.ts, src/screens/GridScreen.tsx, src/screens/CombatScreen.tsx, App.tsx, src/screens/LootScreen.tsx, src/components/TetrisGrid.tsx, src/components/EquipmentPanel.tsx

annotations.author: lac advance
annotations.body: BMAD session 3: MF stat line in InventoryScreen (LVL + MF%), DPad center converted to TouchableOpacity with 400ms long-press onWait, floor-scaled potion heal reads effectiveStats.heal

userGuide: UI polish is transparent — the game simply looks better. Tiles show their type at a glance. Monster names are color-coded. Items shimmer by rarity. The dungeon feels dark and atmospheric.

codeSnippets.label: src/theme.ts — color and font constants
codeSnippets.snippet:
export const COLORS = {
  bg:         '#0a0a0a',
  surface:    '#0f0f0f',
  border:     '#1e1e1e',
  text:       '#cccccc',
  textDim:    '#555555',
  gold:       '#c8a020',
  red:        '#e74c3c',
  green:      '#2ecc71',
  blue:       '#5b8fff',
  purple:     '#9b59b6',
  quality: { normal: '#888', magic: '#5b8fff', rare: '#c8a020', unique: '#ff8c00' },
  class:   { warrior: '#e74c3c', rogue: '#2ecc71', sorcerer: '#5b8fff' },
  encounter: { Normal: '#999', Elite: '#3498db', Rare: '#c8a020', Ancient: '#e74c3c', Boss: '#ff0000' },
}
export const FONT = { xs: 9, sm: 11, md: 13, lg: 16, xl: 20, xxl: 26 }
export const SPACING = { xs: 4, sm: 8, md: 14, lg: 20, xl: 28 }

codeSnippets.label: Color-coded combat log line
codeSnippets.snippet:
function logLineColor(line: string): string {
  if (/crit|CRIT/.test(line))           return '#f39c12'
  if (/Victory|XP|LEVEL UP/.test(line)) return '#2ecc71'
  if (/damage|dealt/.test(line))        return '#e74c3c'
  if (/healed|Potion/.test(line))       return '#27ae60'
  if (/Scroll/.test(line))              return '#9b59b6'
  return '#555'
}
